118	/** Options for a plugin entry that registers providers, tools, commands, or services. */
119	type DefinePluginEntryOptions = {
120	  id: string;
121	  name: string;
122	  description: string;
123	  kind?: OpenClawPluginDefinition["kind"];
124	  configSchema?: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema | (() => OpenClawPluginConfigSchema);
125	  register: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
126	};
127	
128	/** Normalized object shape that OpenClaw loads from a plugin entry module. */
129	type DefinedPluginEntry = {
130	  id: string;
131	  name: string;
132	  description: string;
133	  configSchema: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema;
134	  register: NonNullable<OpenClawPluginDefinition["register"]>;
135	} & Pick<OpenClawPluginDefinition, "kind">;
136	
137	/** Resolve either a concrete config schema or a lazy schema factory. */
138	function resolvePluginConfigSchema(
139	  configSchema: DefinePluginEntryOptions["configSchema"] = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
140	): OpenClawPluginConfigSchema {
141	  return typeof configSchema === "function" ? configSchema() : configSchema;
142	}
143	
144	/**
145	 * Canonical entry helper for non-channel plugins.
146	 *
147	 * Use this for provider, tool, command, service, memory, and context-engine
148	 * plugins. Channel plugins should use `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` from
149	 * `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` so they inherit the channel capability wiring.
150	 */
151	export function definePluginEntry({
152	  id,
153	  name,
154	  description,
155	  kind,
156	  configSchema = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
157	  register,
158	}: DefinePluginEntryOptions): DefinedPluginEntry {
159	  return {
160	    id,
161	    name,
162	    description,
163	    ...(kind ? { kind } : {}),
164	    configSchema: resolvePluginConfigSchema(configSchema),
165	    register,
166	  };
167	}

1	import {
2	  AllowFromListSchema,
3	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
4	  DmPolicySchema,
5	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
6	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";
7	import { buildSecretInputSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input";
8	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
9	
10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document for the user.

## Summary Report: OpenClaw Plugin SDK Channel Contract Interfaces

Based on my thorough exploration of the OpenClaw Plugin SDK source code, here's a comprehensive understanding of the channel plugin contract:

### File Locations

The key interface definitions are located at:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` (re-exports)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (entry point helpers)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` (plugin entry definitions)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` (ChannelPlugin type)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` (Core channel types)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` (Adapter interfaces)

### 1. Main ChannelPlugin Interface

The primary interface is `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>` which includes:

**Required properties:**
- `id: ChannelId` - Unique identifier
- `meta: ChannelMeta` - User-facing metadata (label, docsPath, blurb, etc.)
- `capabilities: ChannelCapabilities` - Advertised capabilities
- `config: ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Config management

**Optional properties:**
- `setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter` - Setup flow
- `setupWizard?: ChannelSetupWizard | ChannelSetupWizardAdapter` - Interactive setup
- `pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter` - Pairing for approval
- `security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - DM security policies
- `groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter` - Group-specific settings
- `mentions?: ChannelMentionAdapter` - Mention handling
- `outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter` - Message sending
- `status?: ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>` - Status/health checks
- `gateway?: ChannelGatewayAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Gateway (websocket/polling)
- `auth?: ChannelAuthAdapter` - Authentication
- `approvalCapability?: ChannelApprovalCapability` - Approval handling
- `elevated?: ChannelElevatedAdapter` - Elevated permissions
- `commands?: ChannelCommandAdapter` - Command enforcement
- `lifecycle?: ChannelLifecycleAdapter` - Config/removal hooks
- `approvals?: ChannelApprovalAdapter` - Approval rendering
- `allowlist?: ChannelAllowlistAdapter` - Allow-from management
- `bindings?: ChannelConfiguredBindingProvider` - Conversation bindings
- `conversationBindings?: ChannelConversationBindingSupport` - Current conversation support
- `streaming?: ChannelStreamingAdapter` - Streaming defaults
- `threading?: ChannelThreadingAdapter` - Threading/reply modes
- `messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter` - Target parsing and routing
- `agentPrompt?: ChannelAgentPromptAdapter` - Agent guidance
- `directory?: ChannelDirectoryAdapter` - User/group lookup
- `resolver?: ChannelResolverAdapter` - Target resolution
- `actions?: ChannelMessageActionAdapter` - Message actions (send, edit, delete, etc.)
- `heartbeat?: ChannelHeartbeatAdapter` - Health checks
- `agentTools?: ChannelAgentToolFactory | ChannelAgentTool[]` - Channel-owned tools

### 2. ChannelCapabilities

Defines what a channel supports:

```typescript
type ChannelCapabilities = {
  chatTypes: Array<ChatType | "thread">; // "direct", "group", "channel", "thread"
  polls?: boolean;
  reactions?: boolean;
  edit?: boolean;
  unsend?: boolean;
  reply?: boolean;
  effects?: boolean;
  groupManagement?: boolean;
  threads?: boolean;
  media?: boolean;
  nativeCommands?: boolean;
  blockStreaming?: boolean;
};
```

### 3. Plugin Entry Points

Channels must export from their entry point:

**For full-feature plugins:**

Use `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`:

```typescript
{
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  plugin: ChannelPlugin;
  configSchema?: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema | (() => OpenClawPluginConfigSchema);
  setRuntime?: (runtime: PluginRuntime) => void;
  registerCliMetadata?: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
  registerFull?: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
}
```

**For setup-only plugins:**

Use `defineSetupPluginEntry(...)` helper:

```typescript
{
  plugin: ChannelPlugin
}
```

**Required exports from entry module:**
- `channelPlugin: ChannelPlugin` - The plugin instance
- `setChannelRuntime?: (runtime: PluginRuntime) => void` - Optional runtime setter

### 4. Group vs Direct Message Types

Handled via `capabilities.chatTypes` and adapter methods:

**ChannelCapabilities.chatTypes** includes:
- `"direct"` - Direct/private messages
- `"group"` - Group chats
- `"channel"` - Public channels
- `"thread"` - Thread replies

**Group-specific adapter:**
- `groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter` - Resolves require-mention, intro hints, tool policies per group

**Security policies:**
- `security.resolveDmPolicy(ctx)` - Returns policy for direct messages
- Separate handling for group vs DM allow-lists

### 5. Message Chunking/Limits Configuration

Defined in `ChannelOutboundAdapter`:

```typescript
type ChannelOutboundAdapter = {
  deliveryMode: "direct" | "gateway" | "hybrid";
  chunker?: ((text: string, limit: number) => string[]) | null; // Custom chunking fn
  chunkerMode?: "text" | "markdown";
  textChunkLimit?: number; // Default max chars per chunk
  pollMaxOptions?: number; // Max poll options
  resolveEffectiveTextChunkLimit?: (params: {
    cfg: OpenClawConfig;
    accountId?: string | null;
    fallbackLimit?: number;
  }) => number | undefined; // Runtime resolution
  // ... other send methods
};
```

### 6. Key Adapter Types

**ChannelOutboundAdapter** - Message sending:
- `sendText(ctx)` - Send plain text
- `sendMedia(ctx)` - Send media with optional caption
- `sendPoll(ctx)` - Send poll
- `sendPayload(ctx)` - Send formatted rich payload
- `sendFormattedText(ctx)` - Send chunked text
- `sendFormattedMedia(ctx)` - Send media with formatting

**ChannelSecurityAdapter** - DM policies:
- `resolveDmPolicy(ctx)` - Returns policy, allowFrom list, approval channel
- `collectWarnings(ctx)` - Returns validation warnings

**ChannelConfigAdapter** - Config management:
- `listAccountIds(cfg)` - Get all configured accounts
- `resolveAccount(cfg, accountId)` - Resolve account object
- `isConfigured(account, cfg)` - Is account properly configured
- `isEnabled(account, cfg)` - Is account enabled
- `describeAccount(account, cfg)` - Return account snapshot

**ChannelThreadingAdapter** - Thread/reply handling:
- `resolveReplyToMode(params)` - Returns `"off" | "first" | "all"` for threading
- `buildToolContext(params)` - Build threading context for tools
- `resolveAutoThreadId(params)` - Auto-thread ID resolution
- `resolveReplyTransport(params)` - Map thread/reply to transport format

**ChannelMessagingAdapter** - Target parsing:
- `normalizeTarget(raw)` - Normalize input (e.g., strip prefix)
- `resolveSessionConversation(params)` - Parse session grammar (threads, scopes)
- `parseExplicitTarget(params)` - Parse explicit target format
- `resolveOutboundSessionRoute(params)` - Build final routing
- `inferTargetChatType(params)` - Guess if target is user/group/channel

**ChannelGatewayAdapter** - Connection management:
- `startAccount(ctx)` - Start listening (websocket/polling)
- `stopAccount(ctx)` - Stop listening
- `loginWithQrStart/Wait` - QR code auth flow

### 7. Configuration Schema

Optional but recommended:

```typescript
type ChannelConfigSchema = {
  schema: Record<string, unknown>; // JSON-schema-like
  uiHints?: Record<string, ChannelConfigUiHint>; // UI metadata
  runtime?: ChannelConfigRuntimeSchema; // Runtime validation
};

type ChannelConfigUiHint = {
  label?: string;
  help?: string;
  tags?: string[];
  advanced?: boolean;
  sensitive?: boolean;
  placeholder?: string;
  itemTemplate?: unknown;
};
```

### 8. Helper Functions

`openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` provides:

- `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` - Wrap channel entry
- `createChatChannelPlugin(...)` - Helper for chat-style channels (handles DM security, pairing, threading, outbound)
- `createChannelPluginBase(...)` - Base channel setup
- `buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute(...)` - Build routing object
- `stripChannelTargetPrefix(raw, ...providers)` - Remove provider prefixes
- `stripTargetKindPrefix(raw)` - Remove `user:`, `group:`, `channel:`, etc.

### 9. Plugin Registration

From plugin entry's `register(api: OpenClawPluginApi)`:

```typescript
api.registerChannel({ plugin: myChannelPlugin });
```

This can be wrapped with `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` which handles:
- Registration mode detection (cli-metadata, setup-only, or full)
- Runtime injection
- Optional CLI metadata and full-mode registration

### 10. Boundary Rules

As documented in `/src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` and `/src/channels/CLAUDE.md`:

- **Public contracts only**: Plugins import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, not `src/channels/**` directly
- **Type safety**: All types re-exported through channel-contract.ts
- **Evolution**: Additive changes are default; breaking changes require major version
- **Helpers**: Use SDK helpers (not direct reach into core internals)

This interface is designed for:
- Multiple chat backends (Slack, Discord, Telegram, Matrix, Signal, etc.)
- Both sync (direct import) and async (external) plugins
- Flexible threading, security, and approval handling
- Rich message formatting and media support

6. `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` - channel adapter types
7. Any other relevant files in `src/plugin-sdk/` that relate to channels

I need to understand:

- How a channel plugin registers itself (what functions/exports are required)

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document for the user.

## Summary Report: OpenClaw Plugin SDK Channel Contract Interfaces

Based on my thorough exploration of the OpenClaw Plugin SDK source code, here's a comprehensive understanding of the channel plugin contract:

### File Locations

The key interface definitions are located at:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` (re-exports)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (entry point helpers)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` (plugin entry definitions)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` (ChannelPlugin type)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` (Core channel types)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775490337022-q4pt1w/src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` (Adapter interfaces)

### 1. Main ChannelPlugin Interface

The primary interface is `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>` which includes:

**Required properties:**
- `id: ChannelId` - Unique identifier
- `meta: ChannelMeta` - User-facing metadata (label, docsPath, blurb, etc.)
- `capabilities: ChannelCapabilities` - Advertised capabilities
- `config: ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Config management

**Optional properties:**
- `setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter` - Setup flow
- `setupWizard?: ChannelSetupWizard | ChannelSetupWizardAdapter` - Interactive setup
- `pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter` - Pairing for approval
- `security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - DM security policies
- `groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter` - Group-specific settings
- `mentions?: ChannelMentionAdapter` - Mention handling
- `outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter` - Message sending
- `status?: ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>` - Status/health checks
- `gateway?: ChannelGatewayAdapter<ResolvedAccount>` - Gateway (websocket/polling)
- `auth?: ChannelAuthAdapter` - Authentication
- `approvalCapability?: ChannelApprovalCapability` - Approval handling
- `elevated?: ChannelElevatedAdapter` - Elevated permissions
- `commands?: ChannelCommandAdapter` - Command enforcement
- `lifecycle?: ChannelLifecycleAdapter` - Config/removal hooks
- `approvals?: ChannelApprovalAdapter` - Approval rendering
- `allowlist?: ChannelAllowlistAdapter` - Allow-from management
- `bindings?: ChannelConfiguredBindingProvider` - Conversation bindings
- `conversationBindings?: ChannelConversationBindingSupport` - Current conversation support
- `streaming?: ChannelStreamingAdapter` - Streaming defaults
- `threading?: ChannelThreadingAdapter` - Threading/reply modes
- `messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter` - Target parsing and routing
- `agentPrompt?: ChannelAgentPromptAdapter` - Agent guidance
- `directory?: ChannelDirectoryAdapter` - User/group lookup
- `resolver?: ChannelResolverAdapter` - Target resolution
- `actions?: ChannelMessageActionAdapter` - Message actions (send, edit, delete, etc.)
- `heartbeat?: ChannelHeartbeatAdapter` - Health checks
- `agentTools?: ChannelAgentToolFactory | ChannelAgentTool[]` - Channel-owned tools

### 2. ChannelCapabilities

Defines what a channel supports:

```typescript
type ChannelCapabilities = {
  chatTypes: Array<ChatType | "thread">; // "direct", "group", "channel", "thread"
  polls?: boolean;
  reactions?: boolean;
  edit?: boolean;
  unsend?: boolean;
  reply?: boolean;
  effects?: boolean;
  groupManagement?: boolean;
  threads?: boolean;
  media?: boolean;
  nativeCommands?: boolean;
  blockStreaming?: boolean;
};
```

### 3. Plugin Entry Points

Channels must export from their entry point:

**For full-feature plugins:**

Use `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`:

```typescript
{
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  plugin: ChannelPlugin;
  configSchema?: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema | (() => OpenClawPluginConfigSchema);
  setRuntime?: (runtime: PluginRuntime) => void;
  registerCliMetadata?: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
  registerFull?: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
}
```

**For setup-only plugins:**

Use `defineSetupPluginEntry(...)` helper:

```typescript
{
  plugin: ChannelPlugin
}
```

**Required exports from entry module:**
- `channelPlugin: ChannelPlugin` - The plugin instance
- `setChannelRuntime?: (runtime: PluginRuntime) => void` - Optional runtime setter

### 4. Group vs Direct Message Types

Handled via `capabilities.chatTypes` and adapter methods:

**ChannelCapabilities.chatTypes** includes:
- `"direct"` - Direct/private messages
- `"group"` - Group chats
- `"channel"` - Public channels
- `"thread"` - Thread replies

**Group-specific adapter:**
- `groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter` - Resolves require-mention, intro hints, tool policies per group

**Security policies:**
- `security.resolveDmPolicy(ctx)` - Returns policy for direct messages
- Separate handling for group vs DM allow-lists

### 5. Message Chunking/Limits Configuration

Defined in `ChannelOutboundAdapter`:

```typescript
type ChannelOutboundAdapter = {
  deliveryMode: "direct" | "gateway" | "hybrid";
  chunker?: ((text: string, limit: number) => string[]) | null; // Custom chunking fn
  chunkerMode?: "text" | "markdown";
  textChunkLimit?: number; // Default max chars per chunk
  pollMaxOptions?: number; // Max poll options
  resolveEffectiveTextChunkLimit?: (params: {
    cfg: OpenClawConfig;
    accountId?: string | null;
    fallbackLimit?: number;
  }) => number | undefined; // Runtime resolution
  // ... other send methods
};
```

### 6. Key Adapter Types

**ChannelOutboundAdapter** - Message sending:
- `sendText(ctx)` - Send plain text
- `sendMedia(ctx)` - Send media with optional caption
- `sendPoll(ctx)` - Send poll
- `sendPayload(ctx)` - Send formatted rich payload
- `sendFormattedText(ctx)` - Send chunked text
- `sendFormattedMedia(ctx)` - Send media with formatting

**ChannelSecurityAdapter** - DM policies:
- `resolveDmPolicy(ctx)` - Returns policy, allowFrom list, approval channel
- `collectWarnings(ctx)` - Returns validation warnings

**ChannelConfigAdapter** - Config management:
- `listAccountIds(cfg)` - Get all configured accounts
- `resolveAccount(cfg, accountId)` - Resolve account object
- `isConfigured(account, cfg)` - Is account properly configured
- `isEnabled(account, cfg)` - Is account enabled
- `describeAccount(account, cfg)` - Return account snapshot

**ChannelThreadingAdapter** - Thread/reply handling:
- `resolveReplyToMode(params)` - Returns `"off" | "first" | "all"` for threading
- `buildToolContext(params)` - Build threading context for tools
- `resolveAutoThreadId(params)` - Auto-thread ID resolution
- `resolveReplyTransport(params)` - Map thread/reply to transport format

**ChannelMessagingAdapter** - Target parsing:
- `normalizeTarget(raw)` - Normalize input (e.g., strip prefix)
- `resolveSessionConversation(params)` - Parse session grammar (threads, scopes)
- `parseExplicitTarget(params)` - Parse explicit target format
- `resolveOutboundSessionRoute(params)` - Build final routing
- `inferTargetChatType(params)` - Guess if target is user/group/channel

**ChannelGatewayAdapter** - Connection management:
- `startAccount(ctx)` - Start listening (websocket/polling)
- `stopAccount(ctx)` - Stop listening
- `loginWithQrStart/Wait` - QR code auth flow

### 7. Configuration Schema

Optional but recommended:

```typescript
type ChannelConfigSchema = {
  schema: Record<string, unknown>; // JSON-schema-like
  uiHints?: Record<string, ChannelConfigUiHint>; // UI metadata
  runtime?: ChannelConfigRuntimeSchema; // Runtime validation
};

type ChannelConfigUiHint = {
  label?: string;
  help?: string;
  tags?: string[];
  advanced?: boolean;
  sensitive?: boolean;
  placeholder?: string;
  itemTemplate?: unknown;
};
```

### 8. Helper Functions

`openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` provides:

- `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` - Wrap channel entry
- `createChatChannelPlugin(...)` - Helper for chat-style channels (handles DM security, pairing, threading, outbound)
- `createChannelPluginBase(...)` - Base channel setup
- `buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute(...)` - Build routing object
- `stripChannelTargetPrefix(raw, ...providers)` - Remove provider prefixes
- `stripTargetKindPrefix(raw)` - Remove `user:`, `group:`, `channel:`, etc.

### 9. Plugin Registration

From plugin entry's `register(api: OpenClawPluginApi)`:

```typescript
api.registerChannel({ plugin: myChannelPlugin });
```

This can be wrapped with `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` which handles:
- Registration mode detection (cli-metadata, setup-only, or full)
- Runtime injection
- Optional CLI metadata and full-mode registration

### 10. Boundary Rules

As documented in `/src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` and `/src/channels/CLAUDE.md`:

- **Public contracts only**: Plugins import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, not `src/channels/**` directly
- **Type safety**: All types re-exported through channel-contract.ts
- **Evolution**: Additive changes are default; breaking changes require major version
- **Helpers**: Use SDK helpers (not direct reach into core internals)

This interface is designed for:
- Multiple chat backends (Slack, Discord, Telegram, Matrix, Signal, etc.)
- Both sync (direct import) and async (external) plugins
- Flexible threading, security, and approval handling
- Rich message formatting and media support

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary document.

## Summary: OpenClaw Bundled Channel Plugin Structure

Based on my thorough exploration of the OpenClaw repository, I've analyzed the IRC, Nostr, and Discord channel plugins to understand the complete structure and requirements for channel plugins.

### 1. BUNDLED CHANNEL PLUGINS DIRECTORY

All bundled workspace plugins are located in `/extensions/` with the following channel plugins identified:
- `/extensions/irc/` - IRC channels
- `/extensions/nostr/` - Nostr protocol DMs
- `/extensions/discord/` - Discord channels
- `/extensions/tlon/` - Tlon (Urbit)
- `/extensions/zalo/`, `/extensions/zalouser/` - Zalo
- `/extensions/feishu/` - Feishu
- And many others (20+ total)

---

### 2. REQUIRED FILES FOR EVERY CHANNEL PLUGIN

Every channel plugin must have these files in its root directory:

#### **A. Manifest File: `openclaw.plugin.json`**
```json
{
  "id": "channel-id",
  "channels": ["channel-id"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```
- **id**: Unique identifier for the plugin
- **channels**: Array of channel IDs this plugin provides (usually single element)
- **configSchema**: JSON Schema for plugin-level configuration (often empty for bundled plugins)

#### **B. Package Configuration: `package.json`**
Required fields:
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/channel-id",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw [Channel] channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "channel-id",
      "label": "Display Name",
      "selectionLabel": "Channel Name (description)",
      "detailLabel": "Detail Label",
      "docsPath": "/channels/channel-id",
      "docsLabel": "channel-id",
      "blurb": "Short description for UI",
      "order": 100 (optional),
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true (optional)
    },
    "install": {
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    }
  }
}
```

#### **C. Plugin Entry Point: `index.ts`**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pluginName } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPluginRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pluginName } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPluginRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "channel-id",
  name: "Display Name",
  description: "Channel description",
  plugin: pluginName,
  setRuntime: setPluginRuntime,
  registerFull: optionalRegisterFunction, // Optional for HTTP routes, hooks, etc.
});
```

**Key exports:**
- The main plugin object from `./src/channel.js`
- The setRuntime function from `./src/runtime.js`
- Default export via `defineChannelPluginEntry()`

#### **D. Setup Entry: `setup-entry.ts`**
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pluginName } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pluginName);
```

#### **E. API Barrels (optional but common)**
- **`api.ts`**: Exports public APIs for other extensions to use
- **`channel-config-api.ts`**: Exports channel configuration types
- **`runtime-api.ts`**: Exports runtime types and helpers
- **`setup-api.ts`**: Exports setup-related exports
- **`test-api.ts`**: Exports test fixtures/helpers

These barrel files re-export from `./src/` subdirectories and follow the boundary rule: only promote to public API what's intentionally meant for consumption.

---

### 3. SOURCE DIRECTORY STRUCTURE: `src/`

Each plugin has a comprehensive `src/` directory with these standard modules:

#### **Core Channel Implementation:**
- **`channel.ts`** (CRITICAL) - Implements the `ChannelPlugin` interface
  - Exports `const [pluginName]Plugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount, ProbeType>`
  - Created via `createChatChannelPlugin()` helper
  - Defines all channel capabilities and behaviors

- **`runtime.ts`** - Runtime store management
  ```typescript
  const { setRuntime, getRuntime } = createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("error message");
  export { getRuntime as get[Plugin]Runtime, setRuntime as set[Plugin]Runtime };
  ```

- **`types.ts`** - Type definitions
  - `ResolvedAccount` type (account configuration + resolved credentials)
  - `ProbeResult` type (for status monitoring)
  - Config types extending `AccountConfig`

- **`config-schema.ts`** - Zod schema definitions
  - Validates channel configuration
  - Built with `buildChannelConfigSchema()`
  - Includes optional UI hints

#### **Setup/Configuration:**
- **`setup-core.ts`** - Low-level setup adapters and helpers
  - `updateAccountConfig()` function
  - DM policy and allow-from setters
  - Account name management

- **`setup-surface.ts`** - User-facing setup wizard
  - Exports `[plugin]SetupAdapter` and `[plugin]SetupWizard`
  - Implements interactive prompts
  - Handles user input parsing and validation

#### **Account & Resolution:**
- **`accounts.ts`** - Account list and resolution helpers
  - `list[Plugin]AccountIds(cfg)` - List all configured accounts
  - `resolveDefault[Plugin]AccountId(cfg)` - Get default account
  - `resolve[Plugin]Account(opts)` - Resolve a specific account from config
  - Creates helpers via `createAccountListHelpers()`

#### **Inbound Message Handling:**
- **`inbound.ts`** - Inbound message processing
  - Message normalization
  - Policy application
  - Access control enforcement
- **`monitor.ts`** - Event monitoring and polling
  - Main loop for receiving messages from the channel
  - Connection management
  - Error handling

#### **Outbound Message Handling:**
- **`send.ts`** - Outbound message delivery
  - Send text and media
  - Target resolution
  - Error handling

#### **Supporting Modules:**
- **`normalize.ts`** - Message/ID normalization
  - `normalize[Plugin]MessagingTarget()`
  - `looksLike[Plugin]TargetId()`
  - Allow-from entry normalization
  
- **`policy.ts`** - Permission/policy logic
  - Group policies
  - Mention requirement resolution
  - Tool policies per sender/group

- **`probe.ts`** - Health check/status probing
  - `probe[Plugin]()` function
  - Connection validation
  - Latency measurement

- **`runtime-api.ts`** - Runtime barrel
  - Re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/[plugin]`
  - Provides local extension access to plugin SDK surfaces

---

### 4. CORE INTERFACES THE PLUGIN MUST IMPLEMENT

From `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`, a `ChannelPlugin` must provide:

```typescript
export type ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown> = {
  id: string;                              // Channel ID
  meta: ChannelMeta;                       // Display metadata
  capabilities: ChannelCapabilities;       // What this channel can do
  reload?: { configPrefixes: string[] };   // Config watch patterns
  configSchema?: ChannelConfigSchema;      // Zod schema
  config: ChannelConfigAdapter;            // Config loading/manipulation
  setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;             // Setup flow
  setupWizard?: ChannelSetupWizard;        // Interactive setup
  pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter;         // Pairing/allowlist notifications
  security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter;       // DM policies, security
  groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter;            // Group/channel policies
  messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter;     // Target normalization
  outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter;       // Send text/media
  gateway?: ChannelGatewayAdapter;         // Server-side endpoints
  status?: ChannelStatusAdapter;           // Status queries and probes
  commands?: ChannelCommandAdapter;        // Command handling
  auth?: ChannelAuthAdapter;               // Authentication
  directory?: ChannelDirectoryAdapter;     // List peers/groups
  resolver?: ChannelResolverAdapter;       // Resolve targets
  // ... more optional adapters
};
```

#### **Most Commonly Implemented for Chat Channels:**

1. **`base`** (via `createChatChannelPlugin`)
   - `id`, `meta`, `setup`, `setupWizard`
   - `capabilities`: { `chatTypes`: ["direct", "group"], `media`: true/false }
   - `reload`, `configSchema`, `config`

2. **`pairing`** - Sends approval messages
   - `idLabel`: What to call the identifier (e.g., "ircUser")
   - `message`: Template message text
   - `normalizeAllowEntry`: Parse user IDs
   - `notify()`: Send approval via channel

3. **`security`**
   - `resolveDmPolicy`: Check if sender is allowed
   - `collectWarnings`: Report security issues

4. **`outbound`**
   - `base`: Delivery mode, chunking
   - `attachedResults`: Send text/media functions

5. **`gateway`**
   - `startAccount`: Connect and monitor account
   - Handle inbound messages with `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`

---

### 5. DETAILED FILE CONTENTS EXAMPLES

#### **IRC Plugin Key Files:**

**`/extensions/irc/src/channel.ts`** (357 lines)
- Creates IRC plugin via `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- **base** section:
  - `id: "irc"`, `meta`, `capabilities: {chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: true}`
  - `config`: Uses `ircConfigAdapter` for scoped account access
  - `messaging`: Normalizes IRC targets (`#channel` vs `nick`)
  - `directory`: Lists IRC peers from allowlist entries
  - `status`: Probes IRC servers, tracks connection state
  - `gateway`: Starts `monitorIrcProvider()` to handle inbound messages
- **pairing** section: Sends "Approved" message via IRC
- **security**: DM policy + TLS/NickServ warnings
- **outbound**: Text chunking (350 char limit), media as links

**`/extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`** (50+ lines)
```typescript
export type ResolvedIrcAccount = {
  accountId: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  configured: boolean;  // has host + nick
  host: string;
  port: number;
  tls: boolean;
  nick: string;
  username: string;
  realname: string;
  password: string;
  passwordSource: "env" | "passwordFile" | "config" | "none";
  config: IrcAccountConfig;
};
```
- `listIrcAccountIds()`, `resolveDefaultIrcAccountId()`, `resolveIrcAccount()`
- Merges account config from base + per-account sections
- Resolves credentials from env vars and password files

**`/extensions/irc/src/types.ts`** (101 lines)
- `IrcChannelConfig`: Group-level config (requireMention, allowFrom, etc.)
- `IrcNickServConfig`: NickServ registration options
- `IrcAccountConfig`: Full account settings
- `IrcConfig`: Top-level + per-account structure
- `CoreConfig`: Extended OpenClawConfig with irc section
- `IrcInboundMessage`: Message from IRC
- `IrcProbe`: Status probe result with latency

**`/extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`** (99 lines)
- Zod schemas for all config types
- `IrcAccountSchema`: Full validation
- `IrcConfigSchema`: Top-level validation with accounts
- `IrcChannelConfigSchema`: Final schema with UI hints
- Validation: port range, NickServ email requirement, dmPolicy rules

---

#### **Nostr Plugin Key Files:**

**`/extensions/nostr/src/channel.ts`** (250+ lines)
- Creates Nostr plugin via `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- **base**:
  - `capabilities: {chatTypes: ["direct"], media: false}` (DMs only MVP)
  - `messaging`: Normalizes pubkeys (npub/hex), strips `nostr:` prefix
- **gateway**: 
  - Starts `startNostrBus()` to connect relays
  - Implements pre-crypto DM authorizer (pairing before decryption)
  - Dispatches inbound DMs with `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
  - Replies go through `reply()` callback
- Manages active bus handles per account
- Stores metrics snapshots for status reporting

**`/extensions/nostr/src/types.ts`** (117 lines)
```typescript
export interface ResolvedNostrAccount {
  accountId: string;
  name?: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  configured: boolean;  // has privateKey
  privateKey: string;
  publicKey: string;   // computed from privateKey
  relays: string[];
  profile?: NostrProfile;
  config: NostrAccountConfig;
}
```
- Account resolution from config
- Private key parsing (hex or nsec bech32)
- Default relay fallback

**`/extensions/nostr/src/config-schema.ts`** (98 lines)
- `NostrProfileSchema`: NIP-01 metadata (name, about, picture, etc.)
- `NostrConfigSchema`: Top-level configuration
- `NostrAccountConfig`: Account settings
- URL validation for https:// only (security)

**`/extensions/nostr/runtime-api.ts`** (5 lines)
```typescript
export * from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr";
```
- Thin barrel re-exporting SDK surface
- Aligned with extension boundary rules

---

### 6. HOW PLUGINS REGISTER THEMSELVES

1. **Entry Point** (`index.ts`) calls `defineChannelPluginEntry()`
2. **Plugin Object** defines all capabilities as adapters
3. **Runtime Initialization**: `setRuntime()` called before use
4. **Config Loading**: `config.isConfigured()` checks readiness
5. **Gateway Start**: `gateway.startAccount()` begins inbound monitoring
6. **Message Flow**:
   - Inbound: Monitor loops → `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
   - Outbound: Handler calls `sendText()`/`sendMedia()` via `outbound.attachedResults`

---

### 7. COMMON PATTERNS ACROSS ALL CHANNEL PLUGINS

**Setup Flow:**
1. `setup-entry.ts` → `defineSetupPluginEntry(pluginName)`
2. `setup-core.ts` → Low-level config manipulation
3. `setup-surface.ts` → Interactive prompts
4. `accounts.ts` → Account resolution helpers
5. `normalize.ts` → Parse user input

**Config Structure:**
- Top-level section: `channels.[plugin-id]`
- Base account config (name, enabled, credentials)
- Per-account: `accounts[accountId]` overrides
- Default account resolution with fallbacks

**Account Resolution:**
- Config is merged (top-level + per-account)
- Credentials resolved from env vars, files, or config
- `configured` flag indicates full readiness
- `enabled` flag allows disabling without deleting config

**Message Handling:**
- Normalize targets (`normalizeMessagingTarget()`)
- Check DM policy via `resolveDmPolicy`
- Apply group policies if applicable
- Dispatch inbound via `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
- Send outbound via `outbound.attachedResults.sendText/sendMedia`

**Status Monitoring:**
- `probe[Plugin]()` checks connectivity
- `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter()` builds status summary
- Tracks latency, enabled/configured state, extra fields

---

### 8. KEY SDK IMPORTS EVERY PLUGIN USES

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`:
- `defineChannelPluginEntry()` - Plugin registration
- `defineSetupPluginEntry()` - Setup entry point
- `createChatChannelPlugin()` - Helper to create plugins

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-*`:
- `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter()` - Multi-account config
- `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter()` - Single-level config
- `createScopedDmSecurityResolver()` - DM policy checking
- `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter()` - Status building
- `createChannelDirectoryAdapter()` - Peer/group listing
- Channel-specific helpers (e.g., `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`)

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-*`:
- `createAccountListHelpers()` - List/resolve accounts
- `normalizeAccountId()` - Standardize account IDs

---

### 9. COMPLETE FILE INVENTORY

**Every channel plugin MUST have:**
```
plugin-dir/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          (manifest)
├── package.json                   (with "openclaw" section)
├── index.ts                       (entry point)
├── setup-entry.ts                 (setup entry)
├── src/
│   ├── channel.ts                 (main ChannelPlugin impl)
│   ├── runtime.ts                 (runtime store)
│   ├── types.ts                   (type definitions)
│   ├── config-schema.ts           (Zod schemas)
│   ├── accounts.ts                (account resolution)
│   ├── setup-core.ts              (setup helpers)
│   ├── setup-surface.ts           (wizard/prompts)
│   ├── normalize.ts               (target/entry normalization)
│   ├── inbound.ts                 (message processing)
│   ├── send.ts                    (outbound delivery)
│   ├── monitor.ts                 (event loop)
│   ├── policy.ts                  (permission logic)
│   ├── probe.ts                   (health checking)
│   └── runtime-api.ts             (SDK barrel)
├── api.ts                         (optional: public API exports)
└── test/, *.test.ts               (tests)
```

**Optional files (for advanced features):**
- `channel-config-api.ts` - Config types for external use
- `test-api.ts` - Test fixtures for other extensions
- `session-key-api.ts` (Discord) - Session routing
- `action-runtime-api.ts` (Discord) - Message actions

---

This comprehensive structure provides a foundation for implementing any channel plugin in OpenClaw. The pattern is consistent across IRC, Nostr, Discord, and all other bundled channels.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed summary document.

## Summary: OpenClaw Bundled Channel Plugin Structure

Based on my thorough exploration of the OpenClaw repository, I've analyzed the IRC, Nostr, and Discord channel plugins to understand the complete structure and requirements for channel plugins.

### 1. BUNDLED CHANNEL PLUGINS DIRECTORY

All bundled workspace plugins are located in `/extensions/` with the following channel plugins identified:
- `/extensions/irc/` - IRC channels
- `/extensions/nostr/` - Nostr protocol DMs
- `/extensions/discord/` - Discord channels
- `/extensions/tlon/` - Tlon (Urbit)
- `/extensions/zalo/`, `/extensions/zalouser/` - Zalo
- `/extensions/feishu/` - Feishu
- And many others (20+ total)

---

### 2. REQUIRED FILES FOR EVERY CHANNEL PLUGIN

Every channel plugin must have these files in its root directory:

#### **A. Manifest File: `openclaw.plugin.json`**
```json
{
  "id": "channel-id",
  "channels": ["channel-id"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```
- **id**: Unique identifier for the plugin
- **channels**: Array of channel IDs this plugin provides (usually single element)
- **configSchema**: JSON Schema for plugin-level configuration (often empty for bundled plugins)

#### **B. Package Configuration: `package.json`**
Required fields:
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/channel-id",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw [Channel] channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "channel-id",
      "label": "Display Name",
      "selectionLabel": "Channel Name (description)",
      "detailLabel": "Detail Label",
      "docsPath": "/channels/channel-id",
      "docsLabel": "channel-id",
      "blurb": "Short description for UI",
      "order": 100 (optional),
      "quickstartAllowFrom": true (optional)
    },
    "install": {
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    }
  }
}
```

#### **C. Plugin Entry Point: `index.ts`**
```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pluginName } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPluginRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pluginName } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPluginRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "channel-id",
  name: "Display Name",
  description: "Channel description",
  plugin: pluginName,
  setRuntime: setPluginRuntime,
  registerFull: optionalRegisterFunction, // Optional for HTTP routes, hooks, etc.
});
```

**Key exports:**
- The main plugin object from `./src/channel.js`
- The setRuntime function from `./src/runtime.js`
- Default export via `defineChannelPluginEntry()`

#### **D. Setup Entry: `setup-entry.ts`**
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pluginName } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pluginName);
```

#### **E. API Barrels (optional but common)**
- **`api.ts`**: Exports public APIs for other extensions to use
- **`channel-config-api.ts`**: Exports channel configuration types
- **`runtime-api.ts`**: Exports runtime types and helpers
- **`setup-api.ts`**: Exports setup-related exports
- **`test-api.ts`**: Exports test fixtures/helpers

These barrel files re-export from `./src/` subdirectories and follow the boundary rule: only promote to public API what's intentionally meant for consumption.

---

### 3. SOURCE DIRECTORY STRUCTURE: `src/`

Each plugin has a comprehensive `src/` directory with these standard modules:

#### **Core Channel Implementation:**
- **`channel.ts`** (CRITICAL) - Implements the `ChannelPlugin` interface
  - Exports `const [pluginName]Plugin: ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount, ProbeType>`
  - Created via `createChatChannelPlugin()` helper
  - Defines all channel capabilities and behaviors

- **`runtime.ts`** - Runtime store management
  ```typescript
  const { setRuntime, getRuntime } = createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("error message");
  export { getRuntime as get[Plugin]Runtime, setRuntime as set[Plugin]Runtime };
  ```

- **`types.ts`** - Type definitions
  - `ResolvedAccount` type (account configuration + resolved credentials)
  - `ProbeResult` type (for status monitoring)
  - Config types extending `AccountConfig`

- **`config-schema.ts`** - Zod schema definitions
  - Validates channel configuration
  - Built with `buildChannelConfigSchema()`
  - Includes optional UI hints

#### **Setup/Configuration:**
- **`setup-core.ts`** - Low-level setup adapters and helpers
  - `updateAccountConfig()` function
  - DM policy and allow-from setters
  - Account name management

- **`setup-surface.ts`** - User-facing setup wizard
  - Exports `[plugin]SetupAdapter` and `[plugin]SetupWizard`
  - Implements interactive prompts
  - Handles user input parsing and validation

#### **Account & Resolution:**
- **`accounts.ts`** - Account list and resolution helpers
  - `list[Plugin]AccountIds(cfg)` - List all configured accounts
  - `resolveDefault[Plugin]AccountId(cfg)` - Get default account
  - `resolve[Plugin]Account(opts)` - Resolve a specific account from config
  - Creates helpers via `createAccountListHelpers()`

#### **Inbound Message Handling:**
- **`inbound.ts`** - Inbound message processing
  - Message normalization
  - Policy application
  - Access control enforcement
- **`monitor.ts`** - Event monitoring and polling
  - Main loop for receiving messages from the channel
  - Connection management
  - Error handling

#### **Outbound Message Handling:**
- **`send.ts`** - Outbound message delivery
  - Send text and media
  - Target resolution
  - Error handling

#### **Supporting Modules:**
- **`normalize.ts`** - Message/ID normalization
  - `normalize[Plugin]MessagingTarget()`
  - `looksLike[Plugin]TargetId()`
  - Allow-from entry normalization
  
- **`policy.ts`** - Permission/policy logic
  - Group policies
  - Mention requirement resolution
  - Tool policies per sender/group

- **`probe.ts`** - Health check/status probing
  - `probe[Plugin]()` function
  - Connection validation
  - Latency measurement

- **`runtime-api.ts`** - Runtime barrel
  - Re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/[plugin]`
  - Provides local extension access to plugin SDK surfaces

---

### 4. CORE INTERFACES THE PLUGIN MUST IMPLEMENT

From `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`, a `ChannelPlugin` must provide:

```typescript
export type ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown> = {
  id: string;                              // Channel ID
  meta: ChannelMeta;                       // Display metadata
  capabilities: ChannelCapabilities;       // What this channel can do
  reload?: { configPrefixes: string[] };   // Config watch patterns
  configSchema?: ChannelConfigSchema;      // Zod schema
  config: ChannelConfigAdapter;            // Config loading/manipulation
  setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;             // Setup flow
  setupWizard?: ChannelSetupWizard;        // Interactive setup
  pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter;         // Pairing/allowlist notifications
  security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter;       // DM policies, security
  groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter;            // Group/channel policies
  messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter;     // Target normalization
  outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter;       // Send text/media
  gateway?: ChannelGatewayAdapter;         // Server-side endpoints
  status?: ChannelStatusAdapter;           // Status queries and probes
  commands?: ChannelCommandAdapter;        // Command handling
  auth?: ChannelAuthAdapter;               // Authentication
  directory?: ChannelDirectoryAdapter;     // List peers/groups
  resolver?: ChannelResolverAdapter;       // Resolve targets
  // ... more optional adapters
};
```

#### **Most Commonly Implemented for Chat Channels:**

1. **`base`** (via `createChatChannelPlugin`)
   - `id`, `meta`, `setup`, `setupWizard`
   - `capabilities`: { `chatTypes`: ["direct", "group"], `media`: true/false }
   - `reload`, `configSchema`, `config`

2. **`pairing`** - Sends approval messages
   - `idLabel`: What to call the identifier (e.g., "ircUser")
   - `message`: Template message text
   - `normalizeAllowEntry`: Parse user IDs
   - `notify()`: Send approval via channel

3. **`security`**
   - `resolveDmPolicy`: Check if sender is allowed
   - `collectWarnings`: Report security issues

4. **`outbound`**
   - `base`: Delivery mode, chunking
   - `attachedResults`: Send text/media functions

5. **`gateway`**
   - `startAccount`: Connect and monitor account
   - Handle inbound messages with `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`

---

### 5. DETAILED FILE CONTENTS EXAMPLES

#### **IRC Plugin Key Files:**

**`/extensions/irc/src/channel.ts`** (357 lines)
- Creates IRC plugin via `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- **base** section:
  - `id: "irc"`, `meta`, `capabilities: {chatTypes: ["direct", "group"], media: true}`
  - `config`: Uses `ircConfigAdapter` for scoped account access
  - `messaging`: Normalizes IRC targets (`#channel` vs `nick`)
  - `directory`: Lists IRC peers from allowlist entries
  - `status`: Probes IRC servers, tracks connection state
  - `gateway`: Starts `monitorIrcProvider()` to handle inbound messages
- **pairing** section: Sends "Approved" message via IRC
- **security**: DM policy + TLS/NickServ warnings
- **outbound**: Text chunking (350 char limit), media as links

**`/extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`** (50+ lines)
```typescript
export type ResolvedIrcAccount = {
  accountId: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  configured: boolean;  // has host + nick
  host: string;
  port: number;
  tls: boolean;
  nick: string;
  username: string;
  realname: string;
  password: string;
  passwordSource: "env" | "passwordFile" | "config" | "none";
  config: IrcAccountConfig;
};
```
- `listIrcAccountIds()`, `resolveDefaultIrcAccountId()`, `resolveIrcAccount()`
- Merges account config from base + per-account sections
- Resolves credentials from env vars and password files

**`/extensions/irc/src/types.ts`** (101 lines)
- `IrcChannelConfig`: Group-level config (requireMention, allowFrom, etc.)
- `IrcNickServConfig`: NickServ registration options
- `IrcAccountConfig`: Full account settings
- `IrcConfig`: Top-level + per-account structure
- `CoreConfig`: Extended OpenClawConfig with irc section
- `IrcInboundMessage`: Message from IRC
- `IrcProbe`: Status probe result with latency

**`/extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`** (99 lines)
- Zod schemas for all config types
- `IrcAccountSchema`: Full validation
- `IrcConfigSchema`: Top-level validation with accounts
- `IrcChannelConfigSchema`: Final schema with UI hints
- Validation: port range, NickServ email requirement, dmPolicy rules

---

#### **Nostr Plugin Key Files:**

**`/extensions/nostr/src/channel.ts`** (250+ lines)
- Creates Nostr plugin via `createChatChannelPlugin()`
- **base**:
  - `capabilities: {chatTypes: ["direct"], media: false}` (DMs only MVP)
  - `messaging`: Normalizes pubkeys (npub/hex), strips `nostr:` prefix
- **gateway**: 
  - Starts `startNostrBus()` to connect relays
  - Implements pre-crypto DM authorizer (pairing before decryption)
  - Dispatches inbound DMs with `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
  - Replies go through `reply()` callback
- Manages active bus handles per account
- Stores metrics snapshots for status reporting

**`/extensions/nostr/src/types.ts`** (117 lines)
```typescript
export interface ResolvedNostrAccount {
  accountId: string;
  name?: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  configured: boolean;  // has privateKey
  privateKey: string;
  publicKey: string;   // computed from privateKey
  relays: string[];
  profile?: NostrProfile;
  config: NostrAccountConfig;
}
```
- Account resolution from config
- Private key parsing (hex or nsec bech32)
- Default relay fallback

**`/extensions/nostr/src/config-schema.ts`** (98 lines)
- `NostrProfileSchema`: NIP-01 metadata (name, about, picture, etc.)
- `NostrConfigSchema`: Top-level configuration
- `NostrAccountConfig`: Account settings
- URL validation for https:// only (security)

**`/extensions/nostr/runtime-api.ts`** (5 lines)
```typescript
export * from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr";
```
- Thin barrel re-exporting SDK surface
- Aligned with extension boundary rules

---

### 6. HOW PLUGINS REGISTER THEMSELVES

1. **Entry Point** (`index.ts`) calls `defineChannelPluginEntry()`
2. **Plugin Object** defines all capabilities as adapters
3. **Runtime Initialization**: `setRuntime()` called before use
4. **Config Loading**: `config.isConfigured()` checks readiness
5. **Gateway Start**: `gateway.startAccount()` begins inbound monitoring
6. **Message Flow**:
   - Inbound: Monitor loops → `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
   - Outbound: Handler calls `sendText()`/`sendMedia()` via `outbound.attachedResults`

---

### 7. COMMON PATTERNS ACROSS ALL CHANNEL PLUGINS

**Setup Flow:**
1. `setup-entry.ts` → `defineSetupPluginEntry(pluginName)`
2. `setup-core.ts` → Low-level config manipulation
3. `setup-surface.ts` → Interactive prompts
4. `accounts.ts` → Account resolution helpers
5. `normalize.ts` → Parse user input

**Config Structure:**
- Top-level section: `channels.[plugin-id]`
- Base account config (name, enabled, credentials)
- Per-account: `accounts[accountId]` overrides
- Default account resolution with fallbacks

**Account Resolution:**
- Config is merged (top-level + per-account)
- Credentials resolved from env vars, files, or config
- `configured` flag indicates full readiness
- `enabled` flag allows disabling without deleting config

**Message Handling:**
- Normalize targets (`normalizeMessagingTarget()`)
- Check DM policy via `resolveDmPolicy`
- Apply group policies if applicable
- Dispatch inbound via `dispatchInboundDirectDmWithRuntime()`
- Send outbound via `outbound.attachedResults.sendText/sendMedia`

**Status Monitoring:**
- `probe[Plugin]()` checks connectivity
- `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter()` builds status summary
- Tracks latency, enabled/configured state, extra fields

---

### 8. KEY SDK IMPORTS EVERY PLUGIN USES

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`:
- `defineChannelPluginEntry()` - Plugin registration
- `defineSetupPluginEntry()` - Setup entry point
- `createChatChannelPlugin()` - Helper to create plugins

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-*`:
- `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter()` - Multi-account config
- `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter()` - Single-level config
- `createScopedDmSecurityResolver()` - DM policy checking
- `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter()` - Status building
- `createChannelDirectoryAdapter()` - Peer/group listing
- Channel-specific helpers (e.g., `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`)

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-*`:
- `createAccountListHelpers()` - List/resolve accounts
- `normalizeAccountId()` - Standardize account IDs

---

### 9. COMPLETE FILE INVENTORY

**Every channel plugin MUST have:**
```
plugin-dir/
├── openclaw.plugin.json          (manifest)
├── package.json                   (with "openclaw" section)
├── index.ts                       (entry point)
├── setup-entry.ts                 (setup entry)
├── src/
│   ├── channel.ts                 (main ChannelPlugin impl)
│   ├── runtime.ts                 (runtime store)
│   ├── types.ts                   (type definitions)
│   ├── config-schema.ts           (Zod schemas)
│   ├── accounts.ts                (account resolution)
│   ├── setup-core.ts              (setup helpers)
│   ├── setup-surface.ts           (wizard/prompts)
│   ├── normalize.ts               (target/entry normalization)
│   ├── inbound.ts                 (message processing)
│   ├── send.ts                    (outbound delivery)
│   ├── monitor.ts                 (event loop)
│   ├── policy.ts                  (permission logic)
│   ├── probe.ts                   (health checking)
│   └── runtime-api.ts             (SDK barrel)
├── api.ts                         (optional: public API exports)
└── test/, *.test.ts               (tests)
```

**Optional files (for advanced features):**
- `channel-config-api.ts` - Config types for external use
- `test-api.ts` - Test fixtures for other extensions
- `session-key-api.ts` (Discord) - Session routing
- `action-runtime-api.ts` (Discord) - Message actions

---

This comprehensive structure provides a foundation for implementing any channel plugin in OpenClaw. The pattern is consistent across IRC, Nostr, Discord, and all other bundled channels.

70	  })

// Private runtime barrel for the bundled IRC extension.
// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.

export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  isDangerousNameMatchingEnabled,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmConfig,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  GroupToolPolicyBySenderConfig,
  GroupToolPolicyConfig,
  MarkdownConfig,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);

content.thinking:
Now I have everything I need to create a comprehensive plan. Let me synthesize what I've learned:

**Key observations:**
1. The Nostr plugin is the simplest channel plugin - it uses top-level config (single account), has no complex IRC-like multi-account setup, doesn't have a separate `client.ts` or `protocol.ts`. It's a pure DM channel.
2. The IRC plugin is more complex - multi-account, server-specific protocol.
3. PulseBoard needs BOTH direct and group chat support.
4. The Nostr plugin uses `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter` (single account), while IRC uses `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` (multi-account).


5. Since PulseBoard supports both direct and group messaging, I'll model it after IRC's multi-account structure with scoped configuration to demonstrate the full pattern.

For PulseBoard, I'm thinking through the architecture: users authenticate with API credentials and connect to a server URL, boards function as channels or groups, and direct messages handle peer-to-peer communication.

Now I'm realizing the `runtime-api.ts` setup differs across plugins—IRC and Nostr each have dedicated SDK subpaths, but PulseBoard doesn't. I need to figure out how to handle imports for a plugin without its own dedicated subpath, since the existing patterns rely on re-exporting from plugin-specific SDK locations. I'm seeing the full list of exports from the IRC SDK facade, including functions and type definitions. Since there's no dedicated PulseBoard subpath yet, I should probably model the imports after how Nostr handles it rather than trying to use the generic IRC exports. Looking at the available SDK subpaths, I can see there are specific modules for different concerns like account handling, channel configuration, pairing, and policy management. I need to map which imports from the Nostr channel.ts correspond to these subpaths so I can create the proper runtime-api.ts file for PulseBoard.

Now I'm shifting focus to design PulseBoard itself—a real-time bulletin board where users post to named boards or send direct messages, with a 4000-character limit per message and API key authentication.

I'm mapping out the file structure for the extension, starting with the core files like the plugin manifest and entry points, then the API handlers and configuration, and finally the implementation details across various modules for channels, runtime, types, setup flows, and message handling.

Looking at the types I need to define, starting with the configuration schema for boards and groups.

Defining the Zod schemas to validate the configuration at each level, from individual groups up through accounts and the full plugin config, plus the channel-specific schema builder.

Now I'm setting up the account resolution logic—functions to list available accounts, resolve the default one, and fetch a specific account with its API token either from environment variables or the config itself.

Then I'm creating a runtime store to manage the PulseBoardRuntime instance with getter, setter, and clear functions, and finally re-exporting the necessary types from the SDK subpaths so they're available throughout the plugin.

For the message handling, I'm building utilities to normalize PulseBoardMessaging targets with their prefixes, validate target IDs, distinguish between board and DM targets, and normalize allow-list entries. The send module handles routing messages to either boards or direct messages through the active connection, while the probe module checks connectivity and returns latency metrics. The monitor module tracks the provider's health.

Now looking at the inbound side, I'm processing incoming messages with policy checks and routing them appropriately, then setting up the core configuration patches for account settings like DM policies and allow-lists. For the UI layer, I'm creating setup adapters and wizards that guide users through credential entry and server configuration, and finally wrapping everything together as a chat channel plugin.

The plugin itself needs to declare its base capabilities—direct and group messaging without media support—along with adapters for config management, group handling, messaging normalization, peer/group discovery, and account status. The gateway monitors the provider connection, pairing uses board messages for notifications, and outbound messages are sent directly with a 4000 character chunk limit.

Exporting the public API... I'm looking at what imports PulseBoard needs from the SDK subpaths—things like account helpers, channel config adapters, and policy composers that handle scoped access and security resolution. I'm deciding between having `runtime-api.ts` act as an aggregating barrel that re-exports from multiple SDK subpaths, or having each file import directly from the appropriate subpaths. Since PulseBoard doesn't have a dedicated SDK subpath like the Nostr plugin does, the cleanest approach is to make `runtime-api.ts` aggregate the commonly needed exports so `channel.ts` can import everything from one place.

Now I'm laying out the core implementation decisions: using `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` for multi-account support like IRC, requiring an API token and server URL for auth, prefixing boards with `#`, setting a 4000 character text limit, supporting direct and group chat types, and keeping it text-only without media support for now.

content.text: Now I have everything I need. Let me write the implementation plan.

I need a detailed implementation plan for a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin for the OpenClaw messaging platform. I've already done thorough codebase exploration. Please use the information below to produce a precise, step-by-step implementation plan.

## What is PulseBoard?

A fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform where:
- Users post messages to named "boards" (like IRC channels, prefixed with `#`)
- Users can also send direct messages to each other
- Supports both "direct" and "group" chat types
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- Auth: API token (`apiToken`) + server URL

## Codebase Context (already explored)

### Pattern: Two reference plugins

**Nostr** (`extensions/nostr/`) — simpler single-account DM-only plugin:
- Uses `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter` (single account)
- `runtime-api.ts` re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr`
- No separate client.ts or protocol.ts
- Auth: private key

**IRC** (`extensions/irc/`) — multi-account plugin with direct + group:
- Uses `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` (multi-account)
- `runtime-api.ts` re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`
- Has client.ts, protocol.ts, connect-options.ts
- Text chunk limit: 350 chars
- Auth: host + nick + password

### Key SDK imports available

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`:
- `createAccountListHelpers`, `describeAccountSnapshot`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`:
- `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution`:
- `resolveMergedAccountConfig`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/allow-from`:
- `formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers`:
- `adaptScopedAccountAccessor`, `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`, `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` and `channel-config-primitives`:
- `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `GroupPolicySchema`, `MarkdownConfigSchema`, `ToolPolicySchema`, `requireOpenAllowFrom`, `AllowFromListSchema`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing`:
- `createChannelPairingController`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy`:
- `composeAccountWarningCollectors`, `createAllowlistProviderOpenWarningCollector`, `composeWarningCollectors`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-send-result`:
- `attachChannelToResult`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`:
- `createChatChannelPlugin`, `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `defineSetupPluginEntry`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/directory-runtime`:
- `createChannelDirectoryAdapter`, `createResolvedDirectoryEntriesLister`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/extension-shared`:
- `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`, `buildPassiveChannelStatusSummary`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store`:
- `createPluginRuntimeStore`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing`:
- `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` (also exported here)

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`:
- `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString`, `tryReadSecretFileSync`, `buildSecretInputSchema`, `hasConfiguredSecretInput`, `normalizeSecretInputString`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup`:
- `patchScopedAccountConfig`, `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`, `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`, `applyAccountNameToChannelSection`, `createSetupInputPresenceValidator`, `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `formatDocsLink`, `patchTopLevelChannelConfigSection`, `createTopLevelChannelParsedAllowFromPrompt`, `mergeAllowFromEntries`, `parseSetupEntriesWithParser`, `splitSetupEntries`, `createAllowFromSection`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers`:
- `createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`, `createDefaultChannelRuntimeState`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-runtime`:
- `convertMarkdownTables`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime`:
- `resolveMarkdownTableMode`, `type OpenClawConfig`, `type PluginRuntime`, `type DmPolicy`, `type GroupPolicy`, `type MarkdownConfig`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-status`:
- `type BaseProbeResult`

From `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod`:
- `z`

### Known IRC channel.ts patterns (exact code):

**ircConfigAdapter** (createScopedChannelConfigAdapter):
```typescript
const ircConfigAdapter = createScopedChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedIrcAccount, ResolvedIrcAccount, CoreConfig>({
  sectionKey: "irc",
  listAccountIds: listIrcAccountIds,
  resolveAccount: adaptScopedAccountAccessor(resolveIrcAccount),
  defaultAccountId: resolveDefaultIrcAccountId,
  clearBaseFields: ["name", "host", "port", "tls", "nick", "username", "realname", "password", "passwordFile", "channels"],
  resolveAllowFrom: (account: ResolvedIrcAccount) => account.config.allowFrom,
  formatAllowFrom: (allowFrom) => formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries({ allowFrom, normalizeEntry: normalizeIrcAllowEntry }),
  resolveDefaultTo: (account: ResolvedIrcAccount) => account.config.defaultTo,
});
```

**IRC outbound** (exact code):
```typescript
outbound: {
  base: {
    deliveryMode: "direct",
    chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
    chunkerMode: "markdown",
    textChunkLimit: 350,
  },
  attachedResults: {
    channel: "irc",
    sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) => await sendMessageIrc(to, text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo }),
    sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) => await sendMessageIrc(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo }),
  },
},
```

**IRC gateway.startAccount** pattern:
```typescript
gateway: {
  startAccount: async (ctx) => {
    // validates configured, logs start
    await runStoppablePassiveMonitor({
      abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal,
      start: async () => await monitorIrcProvider({ accountId, config: ctx.cfg, runtime: ctx.runtime, abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal, statusSink }),
    });
  },
},
```

**IRC account resolution** (accounts.ts) pattern:
```typescript
const { listAccountIds: listIrcAccountIds, resolveDefaultAccountId: resolveDefaultIrcAccountId } = createAccountListHelpers("irc", { normalizeAccountId });

function resolveIrcAccount(params: { cfg: CoreConfig; accountId?: string | null }): ResolvedIrcAccount {
  // merge config, resolve env vars, return ResolvedIrcAccount
  configured: Boolean(host && nick),
}
```

### Constraint: Do NOT modify any existing files

Only create new files under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Since `pnpm-workspace.yaml` includes `extensions/*`, the new package is automatically a workspace member.

## Your task

Produce a detailed implementation plan with:
1. The exact file structure (all files to create)
2. For each file: exact imports, types, function signatures, and key implementation details
3. How PulseBoard-specific concepts map to the OpenClaw channel plugin API
4. Test strategy

## PulseBoard Design Decisions
- Plugin ID: `pulseboard`
- Package name: `@openclaw/pulseboard`
- Multi-account (like IRC) using `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`
- Boards are group targets, prefixed with `#`
- Direct messages use usernames like `@alice` or plain `alice`
- Config path: `channels.pulseboard`
- Env var: `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` (default account only)
- Required for configured: `serverUrl` AND `apiToken`
- Chat types: `["direct", "group"]`
- Text chunk limit: 4000 chars
- Media: false
- No TLS warning needed
- Simple probe: HTTP GET to `<serverUrl>/health`

Please provide a complete, precise implementation plan.

    "text": "I now have all the information needed to produce a comprehensive, precise implementation plan. Here it is:

---

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Detailed Implementation Plan

### Overview

PulseBoard is a multi-account channel plugin modeled after the IRC plugin. It uses `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`, supports `"direct"` and `"group"` chat types, authenticates via `apiToken` + `serverUrl`, and probes via HTTP GET to `<serverUrl>/health`. Boards are `#`-prefixed group targets; DMs use plain usernames.

---

### 1. File Structure

I need a detailed implementation plan for a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel plugin for the OpenClaw messaging platform. I've already done thorough codebase exploration. Please use the information below to produce a precise, step-by-step implementation plan.

## What is PulseBoard?

A fictional real-time bulletin board messaging platform where:
- Users post messages to named "boards" (like IRC channels, prefixed with `#`)
- Users can also send direct messages to each other
- Supports both "direct" and "group" chat types
- 4000-character text chunk limit
- Auth: API token (`apiToken`) + server URL

## Codebase Context (already explored)

### Pattern: Two reference plugins

**Nostr** (`extensions/nostr/`) — simpler single-account DM-only plugin:
- Uses `createTopLevelChannelConfigAdapter` (single account)
- `runtime-api.ts` re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/nostr`
- No separate client.ts or protocol.ts
- Auth: private key

**IRC** (`extensions/irc/`) — multi-account plugin with direct + group:
- Uses `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter` (multi-account)
- `runtime-api.ts` re-exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`
- Has client.ts, protocol.ts, connect-options.ts
- Text chunk limit: 350 chars
- Auth: host + nick + password

### Known IRC channel.ts patterns (exact code):

**ircConfigAdapter** (createScopedChannelConfigAdapter):
```typescript
const ircConfigAdapter = createScopedChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedIrcAccount, ResolvedIrcAccount, CoreConfig>({
  sectionKey: "irc",
  listAccountIds: listIrcAccountIds,
  resolveAccount: adaptScopedAccountAccessor(resolveIrcAccount),
  defaultAccountId: resolveDefaultIrcAccountId,
  clearBaseFields: ["name", "host", "port", "tls", "nick", "username", "realname", "password", "passwordFile", "channels"],
  resolveAllowFrom: (account: ResolvedIrcAccount) => account.config.allowFrom,
  formatAllowFrom: (allowFrom) => formatNormalizedAllowFromEntries({ allowFrom, normalizeEntry: normalizeIrcAllowEntry }),
  resolveDefaultTo: (account: ResolvedIrcAccount) => account.config.defaultTo,
});
```

**IRC outbound** (exact code):
```typescript
outbound: {
  base: {
    deliveryMode: "direct",
    chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
    chunkerMode: "markdown",
    textChunkLimit: 350,
  },
  attachedResults: {
    channel: "irc",
    sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) => await sendMessageIrc(to, text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo }),
    sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) => await sendMessageIrc(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo }),
  },
},
```

**IRC gateway.startAccount** pattern:
```typescript
gateway: {
  startAccount: async (ctx) => {
    // validates configured, logs start
    await runStoppablePassiveMonitor({
      abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal,
      start: async () => await monitorIrcProvider({ accountId, config: ctx.cfg, runtime: ctx.runtime, abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal, statusSink }),
    });
  },
},
```

**IRC account resolution** (accounts.ts) pattern:
```typescript
const { listAccountIds: listIrcAccountIds, resolveDefaultAccountId: resolveDefaultIrcAccountId } = createAccountListHelpers("irc", { normalizeAccountId });

function resolveIrcAccount(params: { cfg: CoreConfig; accountId?: string | null }): ResolvedIrcAccount {
  // merge config, resolve env vars, return ResolvedIrcAccount
  configured: Boolean(host && nick),
}
```

### Constraint: Do NOT modify any existing files

Only create new files under `extensions/pulseboard/`. Since `pnpm-workspace.yaml` includes `extensions/*`, the new package is automatically a workspace member.

## Your task

Produce a detailed implementation plan with:
1. The exact file structure (all files to create)
2. For each file: exact imports, types, function signatures, and key implementation details
3. How PulseBoard-specific concepts map to the OpenClaw channel plugin API
4. Test strategy

## PulseBoard Design Decisions
- Plugin ID: `pulseboard`
- Package name: `@openclaw/pulseboard`
- Multi-account (like IRC) using `createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`
- Boards are group targets, prefixed with `#`
- Direct messages use usernames like `@alice` or plain `alice`
- Config path: `channels.pulseboard`
- Env var: `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` (default account only)
- Required for configured: `serverUrl` AND `apiToken`
- Chat types: `["direct", "group"]`
- Text chunk limit: 4000 chars
- Media: false
- No TLS warning needed
- Simple probe: HTTP GET to `<serverUrl>/health`

Please provide a complete, precise implementation plan.

The plan is complete. Here's a summary of what I researched and designed:

## What I Found

**Explored:** The IRC plugin (`extensions/irc/`) as the primary reference — it's a multi-account plugin with both direct and group support, which best matches PulseBoard's requirements. Also studied the Nostr plugin for its simpler single-account pattern and Telegram for the most feature-rich example.

**Key insight:** The `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` composite subpath exports many runtime helpers used by `extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts`. Since PulseBoard won't have a dedicated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` subpath, the plan flags this as an item to resolve at implementation time by finding the correct generic subpaths (likely `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-plugin-common`).

## The Plan

**20 files** to create under `extensions/pulseboard/`, all self-contained (no existing files touched). The workspace picks it up automatically via `pnpm-workspace.yaml`'s `extensions/*` glob.

**Architecture mirrors IRC exactly** with PulseBoard-specific adaptations:
- Auth: `apiToken` + `serverUrl` (vs IRC's `nick` + `host`)
- Boards: `#board-name` prefix (vs IRC's `#channel`)
- DMs: plain `username`/`@username` (vs IRC's `nick`)
- Probe: HTTP GET to `<serverUrl>/health` (vs IRC's TCP connect)
- Chunk limit: 4000 chars (vs IRC's 350)
- Monitor: simulated WebSocket stub (fictional platform)

The plan covers exact imports, type definitions, function signatures, and SDK subpath resolution guidance for every file.
